articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

images.name: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot
images.caption: Lionel Messi doubles Argentina's lead from the penalty spot

article.headline: Pedri's first Spain goals lead 5-1 rout of Northern Ireland
article.description: Spain strolled to a 5-1 win over Northern Ireland in Palma de Mallorca on Saturday in their final warm-up match before the European Championship starting in Germany next week.
article.linkText: Pedri's first Spain goals lead rout of N. Ireland

images.name: Pedri Spain celeb vs. N. Ireland_20240608 [1296x729]
images.alt: Pedri of Spain celebrates scoring his teamÂ´s first goal during the international friendly match between Spain and Northern Ireland at Estadi de Son Moix on June 08, 2024 in Mallorca, Spain.

article.story:
Spain strolled to a 5-1 win over Northern Ireland in Palma de Mallorca on Saturday as midfielder Pedri scored twice in their final warm-up match before the European Championship starting in Germany next week.

The visitors took a shock lead two minutes into the game when defender Daniel Ballard headed home a long free kick from Caolan Boyd-Munce.

Spain dominated the match, however, and levelled through a Pedri strike from distance in the 12th minute, his first international goal,before Álvaro Morata, Fabián Ruiz and Pedri again put the hosts 4-1 up at the break.

Mikel Oyarzabal sealed the win for the three-times European champions in the 60th minute, bundling in Lamine Yamal's pass.

"Above all, I'm very happy for the match, for the team's reaction. [But also] very happy to make my scoring debut, I was looking forward to scoring here," said Pedri.

"I've worked hard during the season, I've had several mishaps but I've worked hard to get to the end of it.

"With the goal it has been seen that the injury is behind me. Physically, I feel very well, looking forward to the Euros and happy to be here."

The 21-year-old Barcelona player struggled with injury problems last season.

Manager Luis de la Fuente made nine changes from Wednesday's 5-0 victory over Andorra, with Pedri and striker Morata the only players retained in the starting lineup.

Morata, who nodded home to give Spain the lead in the 18th minute with his 35th international goal, drew level with David Silva as Spain's fourth highest scorer.

Spain scored four goals in the first half for the first time since 2023, when they also netted four against Georgia in the Euro 2024 qualifiers.

The hosts were more relaxed after the break but still threatened Bailey Peacock-Farrell's goal as Joselu, Dani Carvajal, Aymeric Laporte and Mikel Merino got game time ahead of the tournament.

Spain start their Euro 2024 campaign next Saturday against Croatia before facing Italy and Albania in Group B.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Spain 0, Northern Ireland 1. Dan Ballard (Northern Ireland) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Caolan Boyd-Munce with a cross   following a set piece situation.
Robin Le Normand (Spain) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Spain 1, Northern Ireland 1. Pedri (Spain) right footed shot from outside the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Nacho.
Goal!  Spain 2, Northern Ireland 1. Álvaro Morata (Spain) header from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Jesús Navas with a cross.
Goal!  Spain 3, Northern Ireland 1. Pedri (Spain) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Nico Williams.
Goal!  Spain 4, Northern Ireland 1. Fabián Ruiz (Spain) left footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Lamine Yamal.
First Half ends, Spain 4, Northern Ireland 1.
Second Half begins Spain 4, Northern Ireland 1.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Jordan Thompson replaces Eoin Toal.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Corry Evans replaces Caolan Boyd-Munce.
Substitution, Spain. Álex Grimaldo replaces Marc Cucurella.
Substitution, Spain. Dani Carvajal replaces Jesús Navas.
Substitution, Spain. Aymeric Laporte replaces Nacho.
Substitution, Spain. Mikel Oyarzabal replaces Álvaro Morata.
Goal!  Spain 5, Northern Ireland 1. Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) right footed shot from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Lamine Yamal.
Substitution, Spain. Mikel Merino replaces Pedri.
Isaac Price (Northern Ireland) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Ross McCausland replaces Conor Bradley.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Dale Taylor replaces Jamie Reid.
Substitution, Spain. Joselu replaces Lamine Yamal.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Brodie Spencer replaces Trai Hume.
Substitution, Northern Ireland. Conor McMenamin replaces Isaac Price.
